title: External Sources Index — AI in Education 2025-2026

_meta.purpose: Indexed metadata for every external source cited in manifest.externalAnchors and across the AI Competency Framework practical layer. This file powers the 'External Frameworks' facet of the references hub (Phase 1f).
_meta.writingNote: Each entry: title + URL + publisher + accessDate + 1-paragraph summary + relevance to Eduversal. URL rot expected — annual review required.
_meta.annualReview: Term 3 each year. Verify URLs still resolve. Update summaries if source has materially changed. Add new authoritative sources that emerged during the year.

cambridgeInternational.categoryNote: Cambridge maintains a live hub rather than a single document. Multiple URLs are authoritative; the hub URL is the entry point.

sources.title: Cambridge International — Teaching, learning, assessment and AI hub
sources.summary: Central landing page for all Cambridge International AI guidance. Includes documents on: generative AI in coursework (Exams Officers Guide Phase 3), getting started with AI in the classroom, preventing plagiarism, AI and the environment.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Primary authority for any AI decision touching Cambridge curriculum or coursework at Eduversal partner schools. School-level AI policy must defer to Cambridge guidance — see redFlagsAndRedlines.json#rl_08.

sources.title: The Cambridge Approach to Generative AI and Assessment
sources.summary: Cambridge's overall position statement on generative AI's role in assessment. Sets the philosophical frame against which specific documents (exams officer guide, plagiarism guidance, etc.) sit.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Read this to understand WHY Cambridge takes specific positions in its more operational documents.

sources.title: Cambridge Drives a New Era of Digital Maturity for the Age of AI
sources.summary: Announcement of February 2026 Primary + Lower Secondary Digital Literacy curriculum refresh. Cambridge explicitly redefines 'digital maturity' for an AI-powered world.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Affects every Eduversal Primary and Lower Secondary class from 2026-2027. Cambridge Coordinator should brief all Primary and Lower Secondary teachers within the same term as the refresh.

indonesiaRegulation.categoryNote: Indonesia has curriculum-level AI regulation (Permendikdasmen 13/2025) and data protection (UU PDP No. 27/2022). NO teacher-conduct AI regulation exists as of 2026-05-17 — Eduversal v1.0 fills this gap.

sources.title: Permendikdasmen No. 13 Tahun 2025 — Koding & AI in Kurikulum 2025/2026
sources.scope: Curriculum scope only — adds Koding & AI as elective subjects (SD kelas 5, SMP kelas 7, SMA/SMK kelas 10). Phased; schools self-assess readiness; NOT compulsory.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Determines whether Eduversal will offer the elective network-wide. Specialist Playbook references this as a potential future curriculum-authoring need.
sources.boundary: This regulation governs WHAT students learn ABOUT AI. It does NOT govern HOW teachers USE AI. Eduversal v1.0 framework fills the teacher-use gap.

sources.title: UU Perlindungan Data Pribadi No. 27 Tahun 2022
sources.summary: Indonesia's comprehensive data protection law, comparable to EU GDPR. Fully enforceable since October 2024. Lembaga PDP (enforcement body) targeted operational 2026.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Critical for all AI use that touches student or staff personal data. Free-tier AI tools that train on inputs create compliance risk. School-tier products with DPA contracts are the safer posture. Red line 1 (no PII in public AI) is anchored here.
sources.dataResidency: No hard localisation mandate, but cross-border transfer burden of proof is high. Conservative posture for student PII: in-country processing or vendor contractual guarantees.

sources.title: UNESCO AI Competency Framework for Teachers (2024)
sources.summary: First global framework defining AI competencies for teachers. 5 aspects × 3 progression levels = 15 competency blocks. Eduversal v1.0 aligns to but doesn't mirror this structure — Eduversal uses 3 levels (Foundation/Practitioner/Leader) × 5 domains (A-E).
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Global anchor for Eduversal v1.0. Mapped block-by-block in manifest.externalAnchors.unescoAiCft.mappingToEduversalV1. Selected verbatim blocks in unesco-ai-cft-2024.json for chip popovers.

sources.title: Recommendation on the Ethics of Artificial Intelligence (2022)
sources.summary: UNESCO's foundational ethics document that grounds the AI CFT. Articulates the human-centred principles cited throughout the AI CFT.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Background ethical anchor. Cited indirectly through the AI CFT.

sources.title: Guidance for Generative AI in Education and Research (2023)
sources.summary: UNESCO's operational guidance specifically for generative AI in education. Cited as a primary source by the AI CFT.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Useful for school-level policy drafting. Leader Playbook references this implicitly.

aiProviderEducatorGuidance.categoryTitle: AI Provider Educator-Tier Products and Policies
aiProviderEducatorGuidance.categoryNote: The 'core three' that are most likely to be Eduversal network-approved for 2026-2027. School-tier products with DPA-grade data handling.

sources.title: Claude for Education (Anthropic)
sources.summary: Anthropic's school-tier product. Signature practice: Learning Mode (Socratic guiding, not direct answering). Partnership with Teach For All covers 63 countries / 100k+ educators.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: One of the three most likely network-approved tools. Strong Bahasa Indonesia per 2025-2026 benchmark. Cautious tone — preferred for HQ curriculum work where errors propagate network-wide.

sources.title: ChatGPT for Teachers (OpenAI)
sources.summary: OpenAI's school-tier product, launched Nov 2025. Free to US K-12 through June 2027. SAML SSO. Not trained on educator data by default. FERPA-aware.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: One of the three most likely network-approved tools. Fast iteration, easiest learning curve for first-time AI users.

sources.title: Gemini for Education (Google)
sources.summary: Google's school-tier product, bundled with Workspace for Education. Operates as 'school official' under FERPA. COPPA-aware. No training on Workspace-for-Education data.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Best fit if school is already on Google Workspace. Strongest Bahasa Indonesia quality of the three per 2025-2026 Artificial Analysis Multilingual Index.
sources.dataResidency: Google global infrastructure with FERPA-class contractual protections

sources.title: OECD — What Should Teachers Teach and Students Learn in a Future of Powerful AI?
sources.summary: OECD's flagship 2025 report on AI in education. Headline recommendation: teacher co-creation of purpose-built generative AI. Strong endorsement of human-in-the-loop assessment.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Authoritative source for assessment-boundary discipline (red line 2). Cited in specialist-playbook + leader-playbook.

sources.title: OECD Digital Education Outlook 2026
sources.summary: Annual OECD digital education report. 2026 edition focuses on AI integration outcomes across member countries.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Useful for school leaders benchmarking their AI integration against international peer schools.

sources.title: OECD + European Commission — AI Literacy Framework for Primary and Secondary Education
sources.summary: Joint framework for student AI literacy. Four domains: Engaging with AI / Creating with AI / Managing AI / Designing with AI.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Alternative student framework to compare against Eduversal v1.0 Part 2. Eduversal uses 4 strands (Understanding / Using Responsibly / Evaluating / AI and Society) — overlap is significant but framing differs.

sources.title: World Economic Forum — Why AI Literacy is Now a Core Competency in Education
sources.summary: WEF positioning paper on AI literacy as a core 21st-century competency. Includes the AILit Framework as a downloadable companion.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Useful framing for parent communication and strategic positioning of AI literacy investment.

sources.title: ISTE Standards Refresh — AI Integration
sources.publisher: International Society for Technology in Education (ISTE)
sources.summary: Incremental refresh of ISTE Standards embedding AI. No standalone AI framework — digital citizenship strand carries the heaviest changes.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Reference for schools that follow ISTE standards. Indirect relevance for Eduversal's Cambridge-anchored framework.

sources.title: Common Sense Education — AI Literacy Lessons + AI Basics for K-12 Teachers PD
sources.summary: Free, curriculum-ready AI literacy lessons for Grades 6-12. Teacher PD courses on AI basics.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Useful starting-point lessons that teachers can adapt for Eduversal contexts. Classroom-activities.json patterns draw indirectly from this style.

sources.title: Artificial Analysis — Multilingual Index (Indonesian)
sources.summary: Independent benchmark of LLM performance on Indonesian language tasks. 2026 leaders: Gemini 3.x and Claude Opus 4.6.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Cited in tool recommendations for Bahasa Indonesia work. Annual review of leaders required as model versions change.

sources.title: Qiscus — Indonesian LLM Comparison
sources.summary: Practitioner comparison of major LLMs for Indonesian use cases. Notes: Claude natural and idiomatic; ChatGPT correct but more formal; Gemini stiffer but improving.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Supports tool-recommendation Bahasa Indonesia notes in manifest.

aiDetectionResearch.categoryNote: Critical for Eduversal's EAL student population. Detection tools are unreliable; multiple major universities disabled Turnitin AI detection by late 2025.

sources.title: GPT detectors are biased against non-native English writers
sources.summary: Influential 2023 study finding that 7 GPT detectors flagged 61% of non-native English essays as AI-generated, compared to much lower rates for native English writers.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Foundational evidence for Eduversal's position that AI detection tools are unsafe for EAL student work. Cited in redFlagsAndRedlines + decision-trees + leader-playbook + manifest.toolRecommendations.categories.aiDetectionAndAcademicIntegrity.
sources.implications: Eduversal does NOT recommend AI detection tools for academic integrity decisions. Process-based integrity (version history, oral defence, draft check-ins) is the alternative.

sources.title: NPR — AI Schools Teachers Students (Dec 2025)
sources.summary: Reporting on the unreliability of AI detection in schools and the disabling of Turnitin AI detection by multiple universities.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Confirms the consensus position cited in Eduversal red lines.

practitionerPatterns.categoryNote: Working patterns shared by practising teachers and validated across multiple practitioner reports.

sources.title: Anthropic Education Report — How Educators Use Claude
sources.summary: Anthropic's research on real educator usage of Claude. Key findings: differentiation is the #1 use case (~61%); lesson design, rubric drafting, parent communication, IEP support are the top categories.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Empirical evidence for the workflows prioritised in teacher-playbook + prompt-library. The differentiation focus is directly anchored here.

sources.title: SchoolAI — 7 AI Prompts That Instantly Upgrade Teaching
sources.summary: Practitioner article articulating the 4-part mega-prompt structure (Role + Context + Task + Constraints).
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Foundational pattern used throughout prompt-library.json.

sources.title: Edutopia — Simplifying PBL Planning with AI
sources.summary: Step-wise project-based learning scaffolding pattern. Iterative prompting over one-shot.
sources.relevanceToEduversal: Cited in prompt-library.json under unit-overview patterns.

_indexing.verifiedActive2026_05: All URLs verified at 2026-05-17 ingestion. Annual review (Term 3) required.
knownGapsInIndex: Indonesian academic research on AI in education — thin coverage; add as published.
Religion / Pendidikan Agama AI guidance — no authoritative source identified; gap noted in classroom-activities.json openQuestions.
Local Indonesian edtech AI products (Ruangguru, Pijar Mahir) cited in manifest but not deeply indexed here — relevance to teacher workflow is low (student-platform focus).
